role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Grace Hall Miller's neighbor counts are moderate (peak 85), with Shirley Miller Sherrod (entry 117) as the top match and the broader cluster including Peggy Jean Connor (#96), Geraldine Crawford Bennett, Toni Breaux, and Willie Elliot Jenkins (#46), Nathaniel Hawthorne Jones (#92). The catalog flags a +0.73 reading toward Strategic Vision on the Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision axis and a +1.00 reading toward Collective Discipline on the Individual Conscience vs Collective Discipline axis as the most-distinctive structural reading.
biographical_paragraph: Grace Hall Miller's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 50 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a not-auditable audit tier: the catalog cannot independently certify the transcript against primary sources for this entry, and any specific biographical or quoted claims should be confirmed against external archives before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
